source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: Queens Bus Network Redesign

payload.description: Proposal to redesign the Queens bus network to better match ridership demands while improving service for customers. Simpler, more direct routes, improved bus stop balancing, enhanced connectivity, improved frequency on key routes, and expanded bus priority.

evidence_refs.source_quote: This is a request for the Chair and CEO to authorize holding a public hearing regarding a proposal to redesign the Queens bus network
A phased implementation is currently targeted for mid-2025
the Proposed Final Plan, which was released in December 2023

tool_args.label: New "Rush" route type introduced
tool_args.raw_text: A new "Rush" route type will be introduced. The purpose of Rush routes is to connect quickly between outer-borough neighborhoods and transit hubs with wider bus stop spacing closer to the transit hubs and closer spacing in the outer neighborhoods.

tool_args.label: QBNR timeline event: Existing Conditions report
tool_args.raw_text: An Existing Conditions report was published in August 2019. This report included an in-depth analysis of the following key data elements: population and employment demographics, travel patterns, ridership, bus speeds, and street conditions. The report, along with valuable information gathered from our customers through public outreach, was used to inform the decision-making and overall process that created each version of the Network Redesign plan to date

payload.description: Existing Conditions report informed the Queens Bus Network Redesign
